comment_text.sn36.11:2.1: The Buddha praises the mendicant because he had the critical acumen to notice these apparently different explanations, and, rather than assuming they were contradictory, or just ignoring the problem, he asked the Buddha for clarification. Compare the Buddha’s quite different response at SN 36.19:5.1.
comment_text.sn36.11:2.15: The “progressive cessation of activities” (anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ nirodho), and their “settling” (vūpasama) and “tranquilizing” (passaddhi) are only mentioned here, SN 36.15, and SN 36.17. Compare the nine “progressive cessations” of AN 9.31 and DN 33:3.2.82, but these go even further to full enlightenment, as in the “progressive cessation in a definitive sense” at AN 9.61. | “Activities” (saṅkhārā) are the conditioned phenomena that are active, being both produced by and productive of other conditions. Here they are the “processes” that are stilled in the course of meditation.
comment_text.sn36.11:2.31: This is the same at the corresponding point in SN 36.17:2.7, but at  SN 36.15:1.22 it speaks of the “progressive tranquilizing of activities”.

root_text.sn36.11:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn36.11:1.2: “idha mayhaṁ, bhante, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi—
root_text.sn36.11:1.4: Sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā—
root_text.sn36.11:1.8: Kiṁ nu kho etaṁ bhagavatā sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.sn36.11:2.2: Tisso imā, bhikkhu, vedanā vuttā mayā.
root_text.sn36.11:2.3: Sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā—
root_text.sn36.11:2.7: Taṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhu, mayā saṅkhārānaṁyeva aniccataṁ sandhāya bhāsitaṁ:
root_text.sn36.11:2.9: Taṁ kho panetaṁ, bhikkhu, mayā saṅkhārānaṁyeva khayadhammataṁ …pe…
root_text.sn36.11:2.15: Atha kho pana, bhikkhu, mayā anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ nirodho akkhāto.
root_text.sn36.11:2.16: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā niruddhā hoti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.17: Dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vitakkavicārā niruddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.18: Tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa pīti niruddhā hoti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.19: Catutthaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa assāsapassāsā niruddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.24: Saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpannassa saññā ca vedanā ca niruddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.25: Khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo niruddho hoti, doso niruddho hoti, moho niruddho hoti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.26: Atha kho, bhikkhu, mayā anupubbasaṅkhārānaṁ vūpasamo akkhāto.
root_text.sn36.11:2.27: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā vūpasantā hoti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.28: Dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vitakkavicārā vūpasantā honti …pe…
root_text.sn36.11:2.29: saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpannassa saññā ca vedanā ca vūpasantā honti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.30: Khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo vūpasanto hoti, doso vūpasanto hoti, moho vūpasanto hoti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.32: Paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vācā paṭippassaddhā hoti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.33: Dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa vitakkavicārā paṭippassaddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.34: Tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa pīti paṭippassaddhā hoti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.35: Catutthaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannassa assāsapassāsā paṭippassaddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.36: Saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ samāpannassa saññā ca vedanā ca paṭippassaddhā honti.
root_text.sn36.11:2.37: Khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno rāgo paṭippassaddho hoti, doso paṭippassaddho hoti, moho paṭippassaddho hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn36.11:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn36.11:1.2: “Just now, sir, as I was in private retreat this thought came to mind.
translation_text.sn36.11:1.3: The Buddha has spoken of three feelings.
translation_text.sn36.11:1.5: These are the three feelings the Buddha has spoken of.
translation_text.sn36.11:1.6: But the Buddha has also said:
translation_text.sn36.11:1.8: What was the Buddha referring to when he said this?”
translation_text.sn36.11:2.2: I have spoken of these three feelings.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.4: These are the three feelings I have spoken of.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.7: When I said this I was referring to the impermanence of conditions, to the fact that conditions are
translation_text.sn36.11:2.15: But I have also explained the progressive cessation of activities.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.16: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.17: For someone who has attained the second absorption, the placing of the mind and keeping it connected have ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.18: For someone who has attained the third absorption, rapture has ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.19: For someone who has attained the fourth absorption, breathing has ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.20: For someone who has attained the dimension of infinite space, the perception of form has ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.21: For someone who has attained the dimension of infinite consciousness, the perception of the dimension of infinite space has ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.22: For someone who has attained the dimension of nothingness, the perception of the dimension of infinite consciousness has ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.23: For someone who has attained the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, the perception of the dimension of nothingness has ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.24: For someone who has attained the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling have ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.25: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have ceased.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.26: And I have also explained the progressive settling of activities.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.27: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has stilled.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.28: For someone who has attained the second absorption, the placing of the mind and keeping it connected have stilled. …
translation_text.sn36.11:2.29: For someone who has attained the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling have stilled.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.30: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have stilled.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.31: There are these six levels of tranquility.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.32: For someone who has attained the first absorption, speech has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.33: For someone who has attained the second absorption, the placing of the mind and keeping it connected have been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.34: For someone who has attained the third absorption, rapture has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.35: For someone who has attained the fourth absorption, breathing has been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.36: For someone who has attained the cessation of perception and feeling, perception and feeling have been tranquilized.
translation_text.sn36.11:2.37: For a mendicant who has ended the defilements, greed, hate, and delusion have been tranquilized.”
